title: amphilophium dasytrichum
intro: Amphilophium dasytrichum, also known as the hairy wild jasmine, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil and other parts of South America, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Amphilophium dasytrichum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Amphilophium dasytrichum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
